what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation result for the A2-canvas-desktop.html mockup (the 'Running Chain' workspace). Holds 31 candidate UI-element dossiers reduced to 17 clusters, each with a ui:// address, capability tags, a grounding flag (built/proposed), a how-to/what/what-you-can-do triple, a maps_to_feature inventory id, and a 3-draw jury verdict that sorts each cluster into 'confirmed' (7) or 'flagged' (12).
observations: Top-level ground block describes the screen as a live map of an active pipeline where nodes execute or wait; lists click-to-inspect, drag-to-link, toolbar-mode actions.
Each dossier carries capabilities from a fixed vocabulary: pointable, spotlit, presentable, openable, driven.
grounding is either 'built' (points at a real code region like canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx or NodeShape.tsx) or 'proposed' (mockup-only, no code path).
Jury structure: votes_grounded / votes_ungrounded out of n=3, with unanimous + quorum booleans driving the verdict.

Two flag mechanisms appear: Layer 1 refcheck (deterministic 'feature fabrication' when maps_to_feature is not an inventory id nor 'proposed') and Layer 2 accuracy jury (SOFT, 'variance not error', flags when no quorum agrees the dossier is grounded).
Some clusters are large (RUN-presence cluster_size 4, RUN-force cluster_size 4) collapsing multiple candidate indices into one dossier.
Flagged-for-fabrication features include RUN-force, CAN-modes; the tabbar dossier got unanimous jury approval yet was still flagged purely on the refcheck fabrication of 'CAN-modes'.
standouts: The two-layer judging design: a hard deterministic refcheck floor plus a soft probabilistic accuracy jury, each able to flag independently.
Explicit 'feature fabrication' detection comparing maps_to_feature against an inventory id whitelist or the literal 'proposed'.
Honesty framing in how-to text distinguishing 'built' code-backed elements from 'mockup-only' proposed ones.
conventions: Uses the howto triple (what / what_you_can_do / how_to_change) consistently across every dossier.
Separates results into 'confirmed' and 'flagged' arrays at top level.
Capability and grounding values come from closed vocabularies.
concerns: A dossier can have a unanimous true jury verdict and still land in 'flagged' solely due to a refcheck fabrication (e.g. CAN-modes tabbar) — the two layers can disagree, which a reader must reconcile.
maps_to_feature 'proposed' is used both as a sentinel and as an actual feature mapping, conflating 'unmapped' with 'intentionally proposed'.
notes: This is the desktop-canvas slice of an automated UI-grounding audit: each clickable element is judged for whether its description is truthfully backed by real code, with confirmed vs flagged buckets.
questions: Where is the authoritative inventory of valid maps_to_feature ids that the refcheck validates against?
What downstream consumes the 'flagged' bucket — is operator scrutiny a manual review step or a re-run?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated screen-grounding purpose is exactly what the data structure delivers — a per-element grounding audit with verdicts.
apparent_themes: Truthful grounding of UI affordances to real code paths
intention_signals: Aims to verify that every interactive element on a generated mockup is honestly describable — that its claimed feature and how-to actually map to built code — and to surface the doubtful ones for human review rather than silently asserting them.
novelty: The hybrid refcheck-floor-plus-soft-jury verdict with explicit 'variance not error' framing, and feature-fabrication detection against an inventory whitelist.
contribution: Contributes a worked grounding-audit record for the desktop canvas surface: the address/capability/grounding/feature schema, the two-layer verdict model, and concrete examples of confirmed and flagged elements that a fused system would keep as the canvas-desktop grounding evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A UI-grounding audit result for the A2 canvas-desktop 'Running Chain' mockup, clustering 31 element dossiers into confirmed and flagged buckets via a deterministic refcheck floor and a 3-draw soft accuracy jury.
intention_for_embedding: To verify each interactive canvas-desktop element is truthfully grounded in real code and flag doubtful or fabricated feature mappings for operator review.
